title: ranunculus acris subsp. stevenii
intro: Ranunculus acris subsp. stevenii, also known as Steven's buttercup, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Ranunculaceae family, characterized by its bright yellow flowers. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe skin irritations, reduce swelling, and combat bacterial infections. The plant's health benefits include its ability to support wound healing, reduce pain, and exhibit antioxidant activity, making it a valuable remedy in folk medicine. The herbal preparations of Steven's buttercup often include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are used topically to apply its medicinal properties directly to the affected area.
